- macOS Tahoe is version 26 (not 16) - Apple jumped from 15 to 26
- ubuntu-latest maps to Ubuntu 24.04 (not 22.04), glibc 2.39

* Pin all GitHub Actions to SHA digests for supply-chain security

Security best practice: pin actions to immutable commit SHAs instead of
mutable version tags to prevent supply-chain attacks.

Pinned actions (all verified via git ls-remote):
- actions/checkout@v6.0.2 → de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd
- actions/setup-node@v6.3.0 → 53b83947a5a98c8d113130e565377fae1a50d02f

- softprops/action-gh-release@v2.6.1 → 153bb8e04406b158c6c84fc1615b65b24149a1fe
- Swatinem/rust-cache@v2.9.1 → c19371144df3bb44fab255c43d04cbc2ab54d1c4
- dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable → 631a55b12751854ce901bb631d5902ceb48146f7

Also added explicit toolchain: stable to dtolnay/rust-toolchain steps
(required when pinning to SHA instead of branch name), and added

Add ConfigurationSchema.json for Azure.Core IntelliSense (#57145)

Adds a JSON Schema segment to the Azure.Core NuGet package that provides
IntelliSense for the AzureClients section in appsettings.json. Defines
retry, diagnostics, and azureOptions definitions matching the current
ClientOptions/RetryOptions/DiagnosticsOptions API surface.

Updates DiagnosticsOptions binding to use additive AdditionalLoggedHeaderNames
and AdditionalLoggedQueryParameters with case-insensitive deduplication,
matching the pattern established in System.ClientModel.

Resolves #56780

[backend] refactor job persistence (#307)

* [design] plan files, agentic commit

* [backend] task 01

* Add jobs2 ORM schema and CRUD helpers

- Add backend/src/forecastbox/schemas/jobs2.py with five ORM models:
  JobDefinition (id, version), ExperimentDefinition (id, version),
  JobExecution (id, attempt_count), GlobalDefaults, ExperimentNext.
  Composite FK constraints link ExperimentDefinition and JobExecution
  back to JobDefinition(id, version).  Soft-delete via is_deleted on
  all main tables.

- Update backend/src/forecastbox/db/jobs2.py: import Base and models
  from schemas/jobs2, add insert/get/list/update-runtime/soft-delete
  helpers for every table.  Latest-version and latest-attempt lookups
  use MAX() subqueries and are deterministic.

- Add backend/tests/unit/test_jobs2.py: 12 unit tests covering
  versioning, latest-version/attempt resolution, runtime updates,
  soft-delete exclusion, and ExperimentNext upsert, all using an
  in-memory SQLite engine.

- Update docs/design/jobsPersistenceRevisit/00-progress.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use Codicon.loading instead of spinningLoading for ResourceLabel compatibility

The spinningLoading ThemeIcon (id: loading~spin) gets rendered as a single
CSS class 'codicon-loading~spin' by ResourceLabels, which doesn't match the
'.codicon-loading::before' CSS rule needed for the icon content. Using
Codicon.loading directly produces the class 'codicon-loading', which has both
the icon glyph and the spin animation defined in codicon-modifiers.css.

commit.message:
feat(opencode/plugin): add proactive policy injection hooks (#4)

Reactive enforcement alone relies on the LLM making a mistake first.
Three new hooks push the policy into the model context upfront, so
violations are prevented before they occur rather than just blocked.

## Context

- `experimental.chat.system.transform` — injects `MAKEFILE_POLICY` into
the system prompt at session start
- `tool.definition` — appends `BASH_TOOL_ADDENDUM` to the bash tool
description so blocked commands are visible in the tool list
- `experimental.session.compacting` — re-injects the policy during
compaction to survive long sessions

A side-effect: the LLM now refuses banned commands at the LLM level
(before calling the tool), so the e2e denial tests were updated to
accept both enforcement paths — hook-level deny log or LLM-level refusal
mentioning make/Makefile/forbidden.

Co-authored-by: Emmanuel Roux <15956441+fesaille@users.noreply.github.com>

an SSO assignment to the audit account, they get 'Insufficient
permissions to list objects'.

- Add 'audit_log_auditors' to foundation_pam in platform.hcl
- Wire through organization-trail/terragrunt.hcl inputs

Auditors can now switch to the audit account in SSO to browse the
CloudTrail bucket.

Fix memory leak in corporate actions backfill

- Cache tenacity retry wrapper as self._retrying_execute in PolygonClient.__init__
  instead of recreating a new closure on every HTTP request. Hundreds of wrapper
  objects were accumulating during pagination loops, causing GC pressure and slowness.

- Add limit=1000 param to fetch_dividends, fetch_splits, fetch_short_interest to
  reduce pagination round-trips (Polygon default page size is small).

- Use executemany() for batch inserts in all three backfill_*_for_ticker functions
  instead of a per-record execute() loop.

- Wrap PolygonClient in a with context manager in run_full_backfill so the
  httpx.Client connection pool is always released on exit.

- Add regression tests: retry wrapper identity check across requests, limit param
  assertions on all three corporate actions fetch methods.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
